nameMeanings.name: Steward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

variants.name: Stewart
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

nameMeanings.name: Of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Steward Of Swaffham was born in 1385, in Norfolk, England, his father, Sir John Stewart II, was 15 and his mother, Matilda Kyriell, was 16. He had at least 3 sons and 2 daughters with Cecily Hamerton. He died in 1433, in Swaffham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Steward Of Swaffham est né en 1385, à Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Stewart II, avait 15 ans et sa mère, Matilda Kyriell, avait 16 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles avec Cecily Hamerton. Il est décédé en 1433, à Swaffham, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Steward Of Swaffham 1385 in Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John Stewart II 15 und seine Mutter Matilda Kyriell 16. Er hatte mit Cecily Hamerton mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1433 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Swaffham, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Steward Of Swaffham è nato il nel 1385 nel Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Sir John Stewart II, aveva 15 anni e il suo madre, Matilda Kyriell, aveva 16 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie con Cecily Hamerton. Morì nel 1433, nel Swaffham, Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 48 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Steward Of Swaffham(이)가 1385년 영국 노르폴크에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John Stewart II(은)는 15세였고, 그의 어머니 Matilda Kyriell(은)는 16세였다. 그는 Cecily Hamerton(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1433년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 스와프함에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.
